# Group 1: foobot and barbot share one set of rules.
User-agent: foobot
User-agent: barbot
Disallow: /private/
Allow: /private/public-note

# Group 2: foobot appears a second time. The matcher merges this group
# with group 1 for foobot; barbot is unaffected by these rules.
User-agent: foobot
Disallow: /tmp/

# quxbot has no rules of its own. Because only blank/comment lines
# separate it from the next user-agent line, it MERGES with the
# catch-all group below (google matcher behavior, consistent with a
# greedy reading of the RFC ABNF): quxbot IS subject to /secret/.
User-agent: quxbot

# Catch-all group for everyone not matched above.
User-agent: *
Disallow: /secret/

# True empty group: at end of file, nothing can merge into it, so
# emptybot has no rules and unrestricted access (the '*' group does
# not apply because a specific group for emptybot exists).
User-agent: emptybot
